mnemonics: S.W.A.P. - System's Way to Augment Physical-memory
keyPoints: Swapping is a memory management scheme where a process can be swapped temporarily out of main memory to a backing store (disk) and then brought back into memory for continued execution.
It is used to free up memory for other processes and allows the total physical address space of all processes to exceed the real physical memory of the system.
The major part of swap time is transfer time, which is directly proportional to the amount of memory swapped.
commonMistakes: Confusing swapping (moving entire processes) with paging (moving fixed-size pages).
Assuming swapping is always beneficial; high swap activity (thrashing) can severely degrade system performance.
